signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: One of the most successful sporting clubs anywhere, Barcelona started winning basketball titles in the 1940s, but its first era of international greatness came in the 1980s. A stellar group led by Nacho Solozabal, Chicho Sibilio and Juan Antonio San Epifanio reached the 1984 EuroLeague Final and then won the 1985 and 1986 Saporta Cups before lifting the Korac Cup in 1987. Barcelona also made three straight EuroLeague Final Fours from 1989 to 1991 but lost each time to eventual champion Split. Another trio of Final Four trips in the 1990s also proved fruitless before Barca won a second Korac Cup in 1999. EuroLeague glory finally arrived in 2003 when the great Dejan Bodiroga teamed up with Gregor Fucka, Sarunas Jasikevicius and Juan Carlos Navarro for the club's first continental crown. The 2009-10 season saw Navarro, Ricky Rubio and Pete Mickeal post an incredible 20-2 record on the way to the EuroLeague crown. Three more Final Four appearances in the next four years were followed by a rare slump before Jasikevicius's return as head coach in 2020 sparked an upturn. Barca went on to reach the 2021 Championship Game and Final Fours the next two seasons. Last season, Barca reached the EuroLeague Playoffs and lost against AS Monaco in Game 5 at the buzzer. After two trophyless seasons, Barca should be more motivated than ever to live up to its standards.

heroMedia.title: Dan Oturu, Elijah Bryant lead Hapoel to critical win
heroMedia.summary: Despite a late scratch, two key players took Hapoel to its 18th win

linkedIds.text: FC Barcelona v Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (2026-03-13T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
It was a strange night at Palau Blaugrana, for a game between FC Barcelona and Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv played behind closed doors in which both teams faced last-minute injury problems. Barca was without star power forward Toko Shengelia, while Hapoel arrived in the building without center Johnathan Motley, who had been dealing with an ankle sprain in recent days and could not recover in time for Hapoel's first game in 14 days, further adding to the unpredictable nature of the contest.

Visiting coach Dimitris Itoudis was in uncharted territory, as Motley had taken part in all of his team's 28 EuroLeague games until now. A quick analysis, however, revealed who would become the most likely go-to guys.

During the last three games in which Motley played less than 10 minutes, Elijah Bryant and Dan Oturu had posted really solid numbers. In those games against FC Bayern Munich, Anadolu Efes Istanbul and EA7 Emporio Armani Milan, Bryant averaged 17.3 points, 6.0 rebounds, 4.3 assists and a PIR of 26.0, while Oturu had delivered 13.3 points on 65.5% two-point shooting, along with 6.7 rebounds and a PIR of 15.7.

Sticking with that script, both players delivered again at Palau Blaugrana. Oturu had 11 points during a first quarter in which he appeared to be the only visitor playing with the right level of energy. Hapoel only committed one foul in the first quarter, allowing Barca to hit 10-of-14 two-pointers (71.4%), while dishing six assists with just one turnover.

"We started rusty, let's be honest," Itoudis admitted after the game. "We started rusty and I think that was a lack of official games. But we used it after that very well. In two quarters, we gave up 14 points - the second quarter and the last quarter. The problems for us were turnovers. We turned the ball over too much and missed free throws. But other than that, we had a clear target of what to do."

Everything changed in the second quarter, which Hapoel's second unit opened with a 0-10 run to make Barca call timeout at 26-29. Levi Randolph, Kessler Edwards and Chris Jones came off the bench with the right mindset and once Bryant, Oturu and Vasilije Micic returned to the floor, Hapoel brought out its best basketball.

The visitor subsequently led 40-46 at halftime and quickly extended its margin after the break: Bryant hit a three-pointer, Micic followed that with a driving layup and Oturu's basket made it a double-digit game, 54-64. At the end of the third quarter, Bryant had 19 points, no missed shots, four rebounds, three assists and a PIR of 29.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by 𝗛𝗮𝗽𝗼𝗲𝗹 𝗧𝗲𝗹 𝗔𝘃𝗶𝘃 𝗕𝗖 (@hapoeltlvbc)

Barca only got closer once the outcome was already clear, and the stats make it clear who mattered the most: Oturu finished the game with 22 points on 10-of-11 two-pointers, while Bryant had 21 points, six defensive rebounds, four assists and a PIR of 32. Hapoel's collective defensive effort was also huge in the last three quarters, in which Barca went 17-of-34 inside the arc (50% 2FG) and 2-of-16 from downtown (12.5% 3FG), getting just five assists in the last 30 minutes against seven turnovers.

"We had a good point of emphasis in the paint, good perimeter movement of the ball, almost 20 assists. And I think we were a better team," Itoudis said. "So congratulations to the team, playing and winning on the road, in Barcelona. It says a lot. And the fact that we are staying in the chase of the play-in spot, it says something for this team."

linkedId.text: FC Barcelona v Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (2026-03-13T19:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Dan Oturu, Elijah Bryant lead Hapoel to critical win

heroMedia.title: Nico Laprovittola out for three months with adductor injury
heroMedia.summary: He is expected to miss the remainder of the EuroLeague season

content.text:
[FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fc-barcelona/bar/) will be without guard [Nico Laprovittola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nicolas-laprovittola/006435/) for approximately the next three months after he suffered an adductor tendon in his right thigh.

Laprovittola, 36, has averaged 8.0 points, 1.1 rebounds and 3.8 assists in 20 EuroLeague games this season.

"During yesterday's training session, our player Nico Laprovittola suffered a complete tear of the long adductor tendon in his right thigh," Barcelona confirmed on X. "The player will undergo conservative treatment, and the estimated recovery time is approximately three months. Get well soon, Nico."

[𝐂𝐎𝐌𝐔𝐍𝐈𝐂𝐀𝐓 𝐌𝐄̀𝐃𝐈𝐂]

Durant l'entrenament d'ahir, el jugador 𝐍𝐢𝐜𝐨 𝐋𝐚𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐯𝐢𝐭𝐭𝐨𝐥𝐚 va patir una ruptura completa del tendó de l'adductor llarg de la cuixa dreta.

El jugador seguirà un tractament conservador i s'estima que el temps de recuperació serà… pic.twitter.com/KL6dXFtB86

— Barça Basket (@FCBbasket) March 12, 2026

This is Laprovittola's eighth campaign in the EuroLeague. He made his competition debut with Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz in 2016-17, then played two seasons with Real Madrid between 2019 and 2021 before spending the last five seasons with Barcelona.

articleMetadata.title: Nico Laprovittola out for three months with adductor injury
articleMetadata.description: FC Barcelona will be without guard Nico Laprovittola for approximately the next three months after he suffered an adductor tendon in his right thigh.

heroMedia.title: Milan staves off late Barcelona rally to secure pivotal win
heroMedia.summary: The hosts held on despite a dramatic fourth-quarter collapse

linkedIds.text: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v FC Barcelona (2026-03-05T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[EA7 Emporio Armani Milan](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ea7-emporio-armani-milan/mil/)'s 87-84 victory over [FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fc-barcelona/bar/) moves the Italian team's record to 15-15 and snaps a two-game losing streak as the battle for playoff basketball rolls on. However, it was much harder work than it should have been down the stretch.

Milan set the pace early, racing out to a near-immediate lead and finishing the opening period seven points in front (24-17). Barcelona showed some resistance at the start of the second quarter, but it struggled to cope with Milan's increasingly fluid offense. By the time the teams headed into halftime, the hosts had extended their advantage to 12 points (46-34).

The game slipped further away from the visitors in the third quarter, when Peppe Poeta's side seized full control, dominating on both ends of the floor and stretching the margin to 27 (78-51) heading into the final period. But that's when things started to get interesting.

Barcelona produced an impressive offensive burst late on to cut the deficit to just two points (86-84) in the final seconds through deadly shot-making from [Tomas Satoransky](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tomas-satoransky/002939/), but the comeback came far too late, and they ultimately fell short by three at the final buzzer.

content.title: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan-FC Barcelona | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
"We have to take the positives of this game, forget about the last quarter and keep going," said Milan's [Leandro Bolmaro](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/leandro-bolmaro/008985/) after his team narrowly escaped the dramatic collapse.

Though the Milanese just about managed to hold onto the head-to-head tiebreaker after losing by two in Barcelona earlier in the season, the fans will certainly come away from the game with mixed emotions following a brutally poor final quarter that invited Barca back into the contest. After the game, Poeta remained encouraged by the majority of his team's performance.

"Maybe the best three quarters of the season," said Poeta. "In the fourth quarter, we just stopped playing. We struggled, we missed some wide-open shots, and it was a scary moment. But we managed to win, which is the most important thing. We also managed to get the tiebreaker, which is important. We showed a lack of patience. We can do better, but it's easier to work after a W."

Milan forward [Zach LeDay](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/zach-leday/008987/) played the starring role, scoring 16 points and grabbing 7 rebounds. Despite his major offensive contribution, he was more tuned into the team's defensive display.

"The best play for me was every time we played good defense, got a stop, and ran," said LeDay. "We just played defense, played together, and we will grow together. We have a lot to grow from, but I'm glad we won."

Play of the Year? pic.twitter.com/k7yl7fts1I

— Olimpia Milano (@OlimpiaMI1936) March 5, 2026

Bolmaro also made his mark on the game, recording 13 points, 4 rebounds and 6 assists. [Quinn Ellis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/quinn-ellis/012593/)'s defensive effort paid off as he combined 3 steals with his 5 assists. Bolstering the offense further, [Josh Nebo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/josh-nebo/011199/) (12 points) [Armoni Brooks](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/armoni-brooks/013384/) (11 points) and [Devin Booker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/devin-booker/007056/) (10 points) also finished in double figures.

The home team managed to secure the win despite turning the ball over 19 times. Ultimately, Milan will be relieved to level its record and certainly won't underestimate the importance of the season's final stages.

"It was an important game for us – it was like a final," Bolmaro said. "The last eight or nine games will feel like a final for us. We need to get into the playoffs; that is our goal."

The next test for Milan comes against Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv next Friday, while Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (now 17-13) will aim to end their freefall when they welcome the hosts on the same night.

linkedId.text: EA7 Emporio Armani Milan v FC Barcelona (2026-03-05T19:30:00.000Z)

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Carsen Edwards seals Virtus’s valiant victory over visiting Barcelona
heroMedia.summary: Defense, depth and late‑game shot‑making carry Virtus after a sluggish start

content.text:
A back-and-forth battle was expected in Round 29, as [Virtus Bologna](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/virtus-segafredo-bologna/vir/) and [FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fc-barcelona/bar/) came off semifinal losses in domestic cup action and both teams needed to bounce back. Virtus had the final word, downing Barca 85-80  at home to improve to 13–16. After a slow start, Virtus played its best basketball in the second and third quarters, then held off Barca’s late rally behind an outstanding [Carsen Edwards](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/carsen-edwards/011987/). 

Barca leaned on two former Virtus stars from the jump. [Kevin Punter](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/kevin-punter/009862/) erupted for 12 first‑quarter points on 3-of-3 three‑point shooting. In his first visit to Virtus Arena since joining Barca, [Toko Shengelia](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/tornike-shengelia/001133/) stuffed the opening 10 minutes with 9 points on perfect shooting and 5 rebounds for a PIR of 14. Shengelia was honored before the game and, as always, put emotions aside once the ball went up. Across nearly four seasons with Virtus, he won an Italian League crown and the BKT EuroCup.

content.title: Virtus Bologna-FC Barcelona | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Virtus then clamped down over the next 20 minutes, limiting Barca to 7-of-15 two‑point shooting (46.7%), 7 assists and 10 turnovers. At the same time, Virtus went 19-of-25 on twos (76%) in the second and third quarters, dished 14 assists, grabbed 7 steals and committed just 3 turnovers. [Luca Vildoza](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/luca-vildoza/007947/) reached halftime with 5 steals, tying a club mark that belonged to [Fabio Di Bella](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/fabio-di-bella/tgq/), who had 5 first‑half steals in a 101–91 home win over Union Olimpija on November 29, 2007.

Virtus led 68–57 after three and stretched the margin to 79–68 with just over 4 minutes left. A game of runs felt inevitable after a demanding weekend, and Barca delivered down the stretch: Shengelia and Punter combined for 10 points in a 0–12 burst that [Nico Laprovittola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nicolas-laprovittola/006435/) capped with a floater for a 79-80 Barca lead in the penultimate minute. Just when a fourth straight defeat loomed for Virtus, Alen Smailagic drew a foul and sank both free throws. After a pair of defensive stops, Virtus put the ball in Edwards’s hands; he followed a lane floater with a step‑back jumper that sealed the outcome, 85–80, with 10.5 seconds left.

"It was a good win for us. I think I just try to move on and just try to play the best I can every game, so that's really what I'm focused on," Edwards said after the game. "I just play. That's all I do is just play to have fun."

What a 𝐓𝐄𝐀𝐌 ❤️‍🔥🤍🖤 pic.twitter.com/jUfSmuvd2h

— Virtus Bologna (@VirtusBo) February 25, 2026

Smailagic finished the game with season-highs of 19 points and a PIR of 21. After 10 consecutive single-digit scoring games and an injury, Smailagic has averaged 15.5 points in the last two games. 

"It feels amazing, especially after a couple of months that I didn't play as I should play. It's good to be back on track," Smailagic said. "We still believe we can get that [play-in] spot, so are just going to keep fighting and keep believing."

Edwards led the way with 22 points. Vildoza finished with 10 points, 7 assists and 5 steals. Punter was Barca's top scorer with 27 points, Shengelia added 16 and Laprovittola tallied 14 points and a career-high 12 assists. 

Virtus played three of its next four games on the road before a four-game home stretch. Barca returns to Italy to face EA7 Emporio Armani in Round 30 before hosting Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv in Round 31.

articleMetadata.title: Edwards seals Virtus’s valiant victory over visiting Barca
articleMetadata.description: After both teams entered Round 29 seeking a response to domestic‑cup exits, Virtus Bologna edged FC Barcelona 85–80 at home. The hosts flipped the game with a dominant second and third quarter, then survived Barca’s 12–0 late run. Carsen Edwards (22 pts) hit a floater and step‑back in the final minute to seal it, while Alen Smailagic posted season highs (19 pts, PIR 21) and Luca Vildoza added 10 pts, 7 ast, 5 stl. Kevin Punter (27 pts), Toko Shengelia (16) and Nico Laprovittola (14 pts, career‑high 12 ast) led Barca.

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Spanish Cup: Hezonja's magic lifts Real past Valencia, Baskonia rallies against Barcelona

content.text:
Mario Hezonja hit 7 three-pointers, including a pair in the final 17 seconds of the game, lifting Real Madrid to an improbable 108-106 win over home side Valencia Basket in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday. In the second game, Baskonia erased a 10-point fourth-quarter deficit to edge FC Barcelona 67-70. This is Baskonia's first appearance in the Cup finals since 2009.

### Hezonja caps Real's improbable comeback

Valencia, which hit 7 first-quarter triples and held 34-16 lead after the opening 10 minutes, let that lead slip before halftime, but then seemed to get the game under control in the fourth quarter. Behind Jean Montero, who caught fire, Valencia was up 11 points with under 6 minutes to go, and up 89-99 with 4 minutes left in the game.

Hezonja nailed his fourth three-pointer to start Real’s rally, and his fifth triple cut it to 99-101 with 1:17 to go. But Montero, who scored 14 fourth-quarter points up to that point, nailed a three-pointer that had Valencia up 101-106 with 20.1 to go.

However, Hezonja quickly hit one more three, then Montero made a mistake on an ensuing inbound play followed by Hezonja’s corner triple, his seventh of the night, to put Real up 107-106.

Valencia had one final possession, but Gabriel Deck stopped Montero’s drive to the basket, and *Los Blancos* celebrated a win that seemed unlikely just few minutes earlier.

Hezonja finished with 25 points, Facundo Campazzo had 17, while Walter Tavares and Deck each added 13 points, with Tavares also pulling down 8 rebounds. Montero ended up scoring 19 of his game-high 26 points in the fourth quarter, Kameron Taylor scored 20 while Nate Reuvers had 13 in a loss.

### Baskonia's rallies late against Barcelona

In the second semi, Baskonia also came back to reach the final. Barcelona led 61-51 in the first minute of the fourth quarter after a triple from Tomas Satoransky, but Baskonia rallied with a 2-13 run. Timothe Luwawu-Cabarrot and Rodions Kurucs each hit a triple, before Trent Forrest euro-stepped his way to the rim to give Baskonia 63-64 lead.

A three from Mamadi Diakite and a floater from Gytis Radzevicius made it 65-69 with 2:43 to go, and Baskonia would prevail even though it did not score another field goal the rest of the way.

Barcelona cut the lead with a fastbreak dunk from Shengelia, and Shengelia had a chance to tie the game with 10 seconds to go, but Diakite delivered a two-handed block and Baskonia preserved the lead.

Kurucs led Baskonia scorers with 14 points, Luwawu-Cabarrot had 13, while Forrest collected 10 points, 8 assists and 6 rebounds. Diakite had 10 points, 5 rebounds and 4 blocks. For Barcelona, Shengelia and Nico Laprovittola scored 12 apiece, while Will Clyburn scored 11.

articleMetadata.title: Spanish Cup: Hezonja's magic lifts Real past Valencia, Baskonia rallies against Barcelona

heroMedia.title: Spanish Cup: Baskonia, Barca made it to the semis!
heroMedia.summary: All four EuroLeague teams are in the Spanish Cup semifinals: Real, Valencia, Baskonia and Barca

content.text:
### Punter's late heroics lead Barca past Murcia

FC Barcelona was the last team to reach the Spanish Cup semifinals by downing UCAM Murcia 91-85 at Roig Arena on Friday. Barca is set to face Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz in the second semifinal. Before that, Real Madrid and Valencia Basket are set to battle for a spot in the final. All four EuroLeague teams made it to the Spanish Cup semifinals. [Kevin Punter](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/kevin-punter/009862/) led the winners with 20 points. [Nico Laprovittola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nicolas-laprovittola/006435/) and [Will Clyburn](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/will-clyburn/004888/) each added 14, [Toko Shengelia](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/tornike-shengelia/001133/) had 13 while [Joel Parra](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/joel-parra/007464/) got 10 for Barca. 

Barca erased an early deficit when [Dario Brizuela](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/dario-brizuela/009992/) gave his team an 11-10 lead. Punter had 7 points late in the first quarter to keep Barca ahead, 20-19, after 10 minutes. [Jan Vesely](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jan-vesely/000925/) and Parra combined for all points in a 6-0 run that gave Barca a 28-23 lead. [Myles Cale](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/myles-cale/013269/) struck from downtown to make it a six-point game, 33-27. Murcia found a go-to guy in Dylan Ennis, then Kelan Martin banked in a triple at the halftime buzzer for a 42-44 lead at the break. 

The back-and-forth battle continued in the third quarter. Shengelia and Clyburn each struck from downtown for a 52-49 Barca lead. Ennis got help from Emanuel Cate for a six-point Murcia lead, 56-62, late in the third quarter. Punter had the last four points in a 10-0 run highlighted by Parra's poster dunk. Punter kept pacing Barca and Clyburn soon gave his team a 77-71 lead with over 3 minutes left. Laprovittola struck from downtown and Clyburn dunked to seal the outcome, 84-76, with 2 minutes remaining.

¡GRAN VICTORIA EN EL ROIG ARENA! 🔥

Sumamos un triunfo más y conseguimos pasar a semifinales de la Copa del Rey 2026 💪🏼

Goazen!💥 pic.twitter.com/HgQcDDbxb1

— Kosner Baskonia (@Baskonia) February 20, 2026

articleMetadata.title: Spanish Cup: Baskonia makes it to the semis!
articleMetadata.description: Kosner Baskonia-Gasteiz made it to the Spanish Cup semifinals by topping La Laguna Tenerife 91-81 at Roig Arena on Friday. Baskonia did not trail in this game and relied on Trent Forrest and Markus Howard to make the difference down the stretch. Forrest led the winners with 21 points. Rodions Kurucs and Timothe Luwawu-Cabarrot each added 14, Mamadi Diakite had 13 while Howard had all of his 10 points in the second half.
